definition.description: Fix q with 6|q. Let Delta subset Z be a finite admissible tuple with 0 in Delta and k := |Delta| >= 2. Admissible means that for every prime p, the set {d (mod p) : d in Delta} omits at least one residue class. Let M(Delta) := max Delta.

notation.pi(x;q,a): #{p <= x : p prime, p equiv a (mod q)}
notation.pi_Delta(x;q,a): #{p <= x - M(Delta) : p equiv a (mod q) and p+d is prime for all d in Delta}
notation.F_r(Delta;q): {a in F_r(q) : gcd(a+d,q)=1 for all d in Delta} for r in {1,5}
notation.nu_p(Delta): number of distinct residues in Delta modulo p

setup_1.description: Fix q with 6|q. Let Delta subset Z be a finite admissible tuple with 0 in Delta and k = |Delta| >= 2. Write M(Delta) = max Delta.

objects.Z_Delta(x;q,r): (log x) * (R_Delta(x;q,r) - S(Delta)), centered-and-scaled error for joint distribution
objects.langle F rangle_X: (1/log X) integral_2^X F(t) dt/t, logarithmic average

objects.r_2(a;x): pi_{{0,2}}(x;q,a) / S_5(a;x), twin start-normalized local ratio
objects.r_4(b;x): pi_{{0,4}}(x;q,b) / S_1(b;x), cousin start-normalized local ratio
objects.A_x: off-diagonal operator ell^2(F_5) -> ell^2(F_1), (A_x e_a) = r_2(a;x) e_{a+2}
objects.B_x: off-diagonal operator ell^2(F_1) -> ell^2(F_5), (B_x e_b) = r_4(b;x) e_{b+4}
objects.J_x: block operator [[0, A_x], [B_x, 0]] acting on H

conjectures.statement:
For each fixed q with 6|q and each r in {1,5}, as x -> infty:
(1) Pi_Delta(x;q,r) = (|F_r(Delta;q)| / phi(q)) * S(Delta) * Li_k(x) + O_{q,Delta}(x / (log x)^{k+1}).
(2) Pi_Delta(x;q,r) / S_r^Delta(x;q) = S(Delta) / (log x)^{k-1} + O_{q,Delta}(1 / (log x)^k).
(3) |Pi_Delta(x;q,1)/S_1^Delta(x;q) - Pi_Delta(x;q,5)/S_5^Delta(x;q)| <<_{q,Delta} 1/(log x)^k.
For each fixed q, Delta, r in {1,5}, langle |R_Delta(cdot;q,r) - S(Delta)| rangle_X -> 0 as X -> infty. Quantitatively: langle |R_Delta(cdot;q,r) - S(Delta)|^m rangle_X <<_{q,Delta,m} 1/(log X)^m.

conjectures.name: Multivariate Logarithmic Limiting Distribution
conjectures.statement: Form the vector Z_I(x) := (Z_{Delta_i}(x;q,r_i))_{i in I}. There exists a nondegenerate Borel probability measure nu_I on R^{|I|} such that langle 1_{Z_I(cdot) in B} rangle_X -> nu_I(B). Heuristically, nu_I is Gaussian with mean 0. In particular, each coordinate Z_Delta changes sign infinitely often with positive logarithmic lower density.

conjectures.name: Gaussian moments asymptotics
conjectures.statement: For any even m >= 2: langle Z_{Delta_i}(cdot;q,r_i)^m rangle_X -> (m-1)!! * Sigma_{ii}^{m/2}. Jointly, mixed moments obey Wick's rule asymptotically.

conjectures.name: Explicit Covariance via Dirichlet Characters
conjectures.statement: The limiting covariance takes the form: Sigma_{(Delta,r),(Delta',r')} = Re sum_{chi (mod q)} W_{Delta,r}(chi) * overline{W_{Delta',r'}(chi)} * sum_{rho_chi} 1/(1/4 + gamma_chi^2), where W_{Delta,r}(chi) encodes fiber signature and tuple-local sieve content.
The inter-fiber difference D_Delta(x;q) := R_Delta(x;q,1) - R_Delta(x;q,5) changes sign infinitely often. Its positive and negative logarithmic densities exist and lie strictly between 0 and 1.

conjectures.name: Asymptotic Permutation Law for Pairs
conjectures.statement: As x -> infty, in operator norm on H: ||(log x) A_x - kappa_2 P_2|| -> 0 and ||(log x) B_x - kappa_2 P_4|| -> 0, where kappa_2 = 2 C_2. Deviations are controlled as <<_q 1.

conjectures.name: Sexy-by-Composition and Anti-commutator Centrality
conjectures.statement:
(1) Composition to sexy: ||(log x)^2 (B_x A_x) - kappa_{026} P_6^{(5)}|| -> 0 on ell^2(F_5).
(2) Anti-commutator centrality: The block anti-commutator {A_x, B_x} := A_x B_x + B_x A_x is asymptotically central relative to the permutation algebra generated by P_2, P_4.

conjectures.name: Quasi-factorization Law for Triplets
conjectures.statement: For each a in F_5(Delta_2;q) such that a+2 in F_1(Delta_4;q), the triplet ratio r_{026}(a;x) := pi_{{0,2,6}}(x;q,a) / pi(x-6;q,a) satisfies: [(log x)^2 r_{026}(a;x)] / [(log x) r_2(a;x) * (log x) r_4(a+2;x)] -> kappa_{026} / kappa_2^2.

conjectures.name: Spectral Clustering
conjectures.statement: The spectrum of K_x := (log x) J_x converges (in Hausdorff distance) to the spectrum of the limit operator K. The eigenvalues of K lie on finitely many symmetric pairs +/- kappa_2 zeta determined by the cycle structure of the +6 shift on the residue classes.

notation.description: Notation for a family of admissible tuples.

objects.H_1,...,H_r: admissible k-tuples subset Z with 0 in H_i and k_i := |H_i| >= 2
objects.Pi_H(t): #{n <= t : n+h is prime for all h in H}
objects.J_{H,M}(a): feasibility mask: 1 if (for every prime q|M and every h in H) a+h not equiv 0 (mod q), else 0; for squarefree M >= 2 and a in U_M := (Z/MZ)^times
objects.Delta_AP(x,y;M,a): pi(x+y;M,a) - pi(x;M,a), short-interval prime counts in residue classes
objects.S(H): product_p (1-1/p)^{-k} (1-nu_p(H)/p), singular series, where nu_p(H) is number of distinct residues -h (mod p) hit by H
objects.Delta_vec(x,y): vector in R^r with i-th entry Delta_{H_i}(x,y)
objects.Delta_AP(x,y;M): vector in R^{|U_M|} with a-th entry Delta_AP(x,y;M,a)
objects.B(x): design matrix in R^{r x |U_M|} with entries B_{i,a}(x) := lambda_{H_i}(x) * J_{H_i,M}(a)

conjectures.statement:
Fix squarefree M for which the rows a |-> J_{H_i,M}(a) are not all identical. There exist absolute constants x_0 >= 10, theta in (0,1), K > 0 such that for all x >= x_0 and x^theta <= y <= x:
||Delta_vec(x,y) - B(x) * Delta_AP(x,y;M)||_2 <= K * sqrt(r) * sqrt(y) / (log x)^{k_min}.
Equivalently, for every weight vector w in R^r:
|w * Delta_vec(x,y) - sum_{a in U_M} (sum_{i=1}^r w_i lambda_{H_i}(x) J_{H_i,M}(a)) * Delta_AP(x,y;M,a)| <= K * ||w||_2 * sqrt(y) / (log x)^{k_min}.

definition.description: Let E/Q be an elliptic curve with good reduction outside a finite set N_E. For p not dividing N_E, write the trace of Frobenius as a_p(E) = p+1-#E(Z/pZ) = 2sqrt(p) cos(theta_p(E)) with theta_p(E) in [0,pi]. Fix modulus m=30 and admissible odd residues R={1,7,11,13,17,19,23,29}. For an interval I=(x,x+h] with x^theta <= h <= x (fixed theta in (0,1)), and a finite partition of [0,pi] into measurable bins B={B_1,...,B_s}.

objects.P_{r,i}(I;E): #{primes p in I : p equiv r (mod 30), theta_p(E) in B_i}
objects.pi_g^E(I;i,j): #{primes p in I : p, p+g are prime, theta_p(E) in B_i, theta_{p+g}(E) in B_j}, angle-conditioned pair counts
objects.T_{g;i,j}(I;E): sum_{r in S_g} P_{r,i}(I;E) * P_{r+g,j}(I;E), angle-residue bilinear proxy

conjectures.statement:
There exists kappa_E >= 1, depending only on E (and not on x, h or the bins), such that for any (g,k) in {(2,4),(2,6),(4,6)} and any i,j with pi_k^E(I;i,j) > 0:
kappa_E^{-1} * L_{g,k;i,j}(I;E) <= pi_g^E(I;i,j) / pi_k^E(I;i,j) <= kappa_E * U_{g,k;i,j}(I;E),
where the observable envelopes are:
L_{g,k;i,j} := (mu_{g,j}^- / mu_{k,j}^+) * (A_{g,i} / A_{k,i}),
U_{g,k;i,j} := (mu_{g,j}^+ / mu_{k,j}^-) * (A_{g,i} / A_{k,i}).

Let mu_ST(B) := (2/pi) integral_B sin^2(theta) d(theta) be the Sato-Tate mass (for non-CM E). Then there exists kappa'_E >= 1 such that for each fixed g in {2,4,6} and any bins i,j with pi_g(I) > 0:
(kappa'_E)^{-1} <= pi_g^E(I;i,j) / (mu_ST(B_i) mu_ST(B_j) * pi_g(I)) <= kappa'_E.

Let x -> infty and let a_n >= 0 be weights supported on I_x := (x/2,x] with average 1+o(1): A(x) := (2/x) sum_{x/2 < n <= x} a_n = 1+o(1). Let b_n be a comparably bounded structured approximation to a_n, and set w_n := a_n - b_n. Fix parameters 0 < gamma < 1, 0 <= theta <= 1/2, and 0 < nu < 1-theta. Assume divisor-boundedness: a_n, b_n <<_epsilon x^epsilon, and:
(1) Type I (strong mean-zero in progressions to level x^theta with L^2-saving x^gamma):
sum_{q <= x^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n in I_x, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n|^2 << x^{2-2gamma}.
(2) Type II (bilinear cancellation in balanced ranges):
For all M,N with x^nu <= M,N <= x^{1-nu} and MN ~ x, and all complex sequences alpha_m, beta_n supported on m~M, n~N with ||alpha||_2 = ||beta||_2 = 1 and |alpha_m|, |beta_n| <<_epsilon (mn)^epsilon:
B(M,N;alpha,beta) := |sum_{m~M} sum_{n~N} w_{mn} alpha_m beta_n| << x^{1-gamma}.

conjectures.name: Phase diagram for primes
conjectures.statement:
Assume Type I and Type II as above. If theta + 2nu > 1, then one has the prime asymptotic with a power-saving error governed by gamma:
sum_{x/2 < p <= x} a_p = (1+o(1)) x/log x,
more quantitatively:
sum_{x/2 < p <= x} a_p = x/log x + O(x^{1-c*min(gamma, theta+2nu-1)} / log x)
for some absolute c > 0 (independent of x), provided b_n is calibrated so that sum_{x/2 < n <= x} b_n Lambda(n) reproduces the expected main term up to o(x/log x).

conjectures.setup: Let x -> infty. Let nonneg weights a_n be supported on I_x := (x/2,x], satisfy divisor-boundedness a_n <<_epsilon x^epsilon, and have normalized average A(x) := (2/x) sum_{x/2 < n <= x} a_n = 1+o(1). Let b_n be a structured model with b_n <<_epsilon x^epsilon such that sum_{x/2 < n <= x} b_n Lambda(n) = x/log x + o(x/log x). Put w_n := a_n - b_n.
conjectures.statement:
Fix 0 < gamma_I, gamma_II < 1, 0 <= theta <= 1/2, 0 < nu < 1-theta, and 0 <= kappa < 1.
Type I (L^2 cancellation in progressions up to x^theta with saving x^{gamma_I}):
S_I(x) := sum_{q <= x^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n in I_x, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n|^2 << x^{2-2gamma_I}.
Type II (bilinear cancellation with sparse exceptional rectangles): For every dyadic M,N with MN ~ x and min(M,N) >= x^nu, for all sequences alpha_m, beta_n with ||alpha||_2 = ||beta||_2 = 1 and |alpha_m|, |beta_n| <<_epsilon (mn)^epsilon:
B(M,N;alpha,beta) := |sum_{m~M} sum_{n~N} w_{mn} alpha_m beta_n| <= x^{1-gamma_II}
except on a set E_x of dyadic rectangles with logarithmic-area density:
dens_log(E_x) := |{(log M, log N) : MN~x, min(M,N)>=x^nu, (M,N) exceptional}| / |{all such (M,N)}| <= x^{-kappa}.
Conclusion (Robust phase diagram): If theta + 2nu > 1 + kappa, then there exists c = c(epsilon) > 0 such that:
sum_{x/2 < p <= x} a_p = x/log x + O(x^{1-c*min(gamma_I, gamma_II, theta+2nu-1-kappa)} / log x).
Borderline: At theta + 2nu = 1 + kappa, the same holds with power-saving replaced by a logarithmic loss, assuming mild well-factorability of b_n.

conjectures.name: Bohr-window phase law
conjectures.setup:
Fix 0 < rho < 1/2 and irrational alpha in R, beta in R/Z. For X -> infty define the Bohr-window prime counting function on (X/2,X]: S_{alpha,beta,rho}(X) := #{X/2 < p <= X : p prime, ||alpha p - beta|| < p^{-rho}}. Let a_n be nonneg weights supported on (X/2,X] majorizing the Bohr window indicator, a_n <<_epsilon X^epsilon, with normalized average 1+o(1). Let b_n be a structured approximation with b_n <<_epsilon X^epsilon reproducing the main term. Put w_n := a_n - b_n. Fix 0 < gamma < 1, 0 <= theta <= 1/2, 0 < nu < 1-theta. Assume:
Type I: sum_{q <= X^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{X/2<n<=X, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n|^2 << X^{2-2gamma}.
Type II: For dyadic M,N with MN ~ X and min(M,N) >= X^nu, all alpha_m, beta_n with ||alpha||_2 = ||beta||_2 = 1 and |alpha_m|, |beta_n| <<_epsilon (mn)^epsilon: B(M,N;alpha,beta) << X^{1-gamma}.
Bohr truncation: Approximate 1_{||alpha n - beta|| < n^{-rho}} by a Vaaler trigonometric polynomial of height H ~ X^rho: 1_{||t||<delta} = sum_{|h|<=H} c_h(delta,H) e(ht) + O(H^{-1}), with sum_{|h|<=H} |c_h| << 1.
conjectures.statement:
If theta + 2nu > 1 - 2rho, then there exists c > 0 such that:
S_{alpha,beta,rho}(X) = (2+o(1)) sum_{X/2 < p <= X} p^{-rho} + O(X^{1-c*min(gamma, theta+2nu-(1-2rho))} / log X).
Equivalently, by partial summation:
S_{alpha,beta,rho}(X) = (2/(1-rho)+o(1)) * X^{1-rho}/log X + O(X^{1-c*min(gamma, theta+2nu-(1-2rho))} / log X).
Borderline: At theta + 2nu = 1 - 2rho, one expects only logarithmic savings unless additional well-factorability of b_n is imposed.

conjectures.setup: Fix integer base b >= 2 and digit delta in {0,...,b-1}. Let S_{b,delta} be the set of n >= 1 whose base-b expansion avoids the digit delta. For X -> infty, Pi_{b,delta}(X) := #{X/2 < p <= X : p prime, p in S_{b,delta}}. The set S_{b,delta} has size ~ X^{rho_b} with rho_b := log_b(b-1). Weights and Structured Model: Let a_n be a nonneg digital weight with spectral height H ~ X^{sigma_b} where sigma_b := 1-rho_b = log_b(b/(b-1)). Let b_n be a well-factorable/sieved structured approximation such that sum_{X/2<n<=X} b_n Lambda(n) = C_{b,delta} X^{rho_b}/log X + o(X^{rho_b}/log X). Set w_n := a_n - b_n.
conjectures.statement:
Assume the following hold uniformly over all Walsh frequencies |h| << H:
Type I (L^2 Mean-Zero in Progressions): sum_{q <= X^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n in I_X, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n * omega_h(n)|^2 << X^{2-2gamma}.
Type II (Bilinear Cancellation): For MN ~ X and min(M,N) >= X^nu: B(M,N;alpha,beta,h) := |sum_{m~M} sum_{n~N} w_{mn} * omega_h(mn) alpha_m beta_n| << X^{1-gamma}.

If gamma + theta + 2nu > 1, then:
Pi_{b,delta}(X) = C_{b,delta} X^{rho_b}/log X + O(X^{rho_b - c*min(gamma, gamma+theta+2nu-1)} / log X).

conjectures.name: gamma-shifted Phase Inequality
conjectures.setup:
Let X -> infty. Consider the thin set A_X = {n in (X/2,X] : n = x^2 + (y^2+1)^2 for some x,y in Z}. Let a_n >= 0 be divisor-bounded weights (a_n <<_epsilon X^epsilon) supported on (X/2,X] with smooth cutoffs in x,y at scales X^{1/2} and X^{1/4}, normalized average 1+o(1). Let b_n be a structured approximation calibrated so that sum_{X/2<n<=X} b_n Lambda(n) = C * X^{3/4}/log X + o(X^{3/4}/log X). Put w_n := a_n - b_n. Quartic-frequency control introduces quartic additive twists e(h(y^2+1)^2) with frequency height H ~ X^{1/4}. Assume uniformity for all |h| <= H:
Type I: S_I(X;h) := sum_{q<=X^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n in I_x, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n * omega_h(n)|^2 << X^{2-2gamma}.
Type II: B(M,N;alpha,beta,h) << X^{1-gamma} for all dyadic M,N with MN ~ X and min(M,N) >= X^nu.
conjectures.statement:
If gamma + theta + 2nu > 1, then there exists c = c(epsilon) > 0 such that:
Pi(X) := #{X/2 < p <= X : p = x^2 + (y^2+1)^2} = C * X^{3/4}/log X + O(X^{3/4 - c*min(gamma, gamma+theta+2nu-1)} / log X).

conjectures.name: gamma-shifted Phase Law
conjectures.setup:
Let x -> infty. Consider the thin set A_x := {n in (x/2,x] : n = x^2 + (y^3+z^3)^2 for some integers x,y,z}. Weights: a_n >= 0 divisor-bounded with smooth cutoffs x ~ x^{1/2} and y,z ~ x^{1/6}. Normalized average A(x) := (2/x) sum_{x/2<n<=x} a_n = 1+o(1). Model: b_n calibrated so sum_{x/2<n<=x} b_n Lambda(n) = C * x^{5/6}/log x + o(x^{5/6}/log x). Error Weight: w_n := a_n - b_n. Nested Harmonic Structure via two-level delta-method: n = x^2+t^2 with t ~ x^{1/2}, t = y^3+z^3 with y,z ~ x^{1/6}. Effective harmonic height H ~ x^{1/6+o(1)}. Assume uniformity for all |h| <= H:
(1) Type I: sum_{q<=x^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{x/2<n<=x, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n * omega_h(n)|^2 << x^{2-2gamma}.
(2) Type II: B(M,N;alpha,beta,h) := |sum_{m~M} sum_{n~N} w_{mn} * omega_h(mn) alpha_m beta_n| << x^{1-gamma}.
conjectures.statement:
If gamma + theta + 2nu > 1, then there exists c = c(epsilon) > 0 such that:
Pi(x) := #{x/2 < p <= x : p = x^2 + (y^3+z^3)^2} = C x^{5/6}/log x + O(x^{5/6 - c*min(gamma, gamma+theta+2nu-1)} / log x).

conjectures.setup:
Fix nonzero a in Z that is not a square. For large X: S_a(X;I) := #{X/2 < p <= X : p prime, exists x in Z with 0<=x<p, x^2 equiv a (mod p), and x/p in I_p}, where I_p subset [0,1) has |I_p| ~ p^{-2/3}. Fourier Truncation: decompose 1_{x/n in I_n} over harmonics |h|<=H_n with H_n ~ n^{2/3}, producing twisted weight sequence with quadratic additive trace-function factors omega_h(n) (Salie-type phases). Structured proxy b_n with sum_{X/2<n<=X} b_n Lambda(n) = (C_I+o(1)) X^{1/3}/log X. Fix 0 < gamma <= 1/2, 0 <= theta <= 1/2, 0 < nu < 1-theta. Assume for all |h| <= X^{2/3+o(1)}:
Type I: sum_{q<=X^theta} sum_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{X/2<n<=X, n equiv a (mod q)} w_n * omega_h(n)|^2 << X^{2-2gamma}.
Type II: B(M,N;alpha,beta;h) := |sum_{m~M} sum_{n~N} w_{mn} * omega_h(mn) alpha_m beta_n| << X^{1-gamma}.
conjectures.statement:
If gamma + theta + 2nu > 1, then for some absolute c > 0:
S_a(X;I) = C_I * X^{1/3}/log X + O(X^{1/3 - c*min(gamma, gamma+theta+2nu-1)} / log X).

objects.parameters: x -> infty, R = x^{1/2-epsilon} with fixed epsilon > 0, factorization R = R_1 R_2 R_3; work with odd squarefree moduli; handle p=2 separately
objects.alpha: triply well-factorable base sequence: multiplicative alpha: N -> R with support on odd squarefree d <= R and alpha = rho_1 * rho_2 * rho_3, each rho_j supported on odd squarefree d <= R_j; for odd primes p <= R_j, rho_j(p) = c_j t_p with t_p := 1/(p-2) and constants c_j > 0 tuned so that sum_{d<=R} alpha(d)^2 ~ 1 (normalization); rho_j(2) = 0
objects.kappa_p(a,b): local coupling kernel for each prime p on a,b in {0,1} (a=1_{p|d}, b=1_{p|e}): kappa_p(a,b) := 1-(1+t_p)(a+b)+(1+2t_p)ab for p>=3; kappa_2(a,b) := 1-a-b. Note kappa_p(1,1)=0 for p>=3; kappa_p(1,0)=kappa_p(0,1)=-t_p; kappa_p(0,0)=1
objects.K(d,e): bivariate coefficient: product_{p>=3} kappa_p(1_{p|d}, 1_{p|e}) for odd squarefree d,e
objects.lambda_{d,e}: mu(d) mu(e) alpha(d) alpha(e) K(d,e) 1_{(d,e)=1} 1_{2 not | de}; equivalently using 1_{(d,e)=1} = sum_{m|(d,e)} mu(m): lambda_{d,e} = sum_{m|(d,e)} mu(m) g(m) f(d/m) f(e/m), where f(u) := mu(u) alpha(u) prod_{p|u}(-t_p) and g(m) := prod_{p|m}(1+2t_p) for odd squarefree u
objects.W(n): sieve weight: sum_{d|n} sum_{e|n+2} lambda_{d,e} for n <= x

objects.triply_well_factorable_sequence: a complex sequence (lambda_q)_{q<=Q} is triply well-factorable of level Q if for any Q_1,Q_2,Q_3>=1 with Q_1 Q_2 Q_3=Q, there exist 1-bounded sequences beta_j supported on q<=Q_j (j=1,2,3) such that for all q<=Q: lambda_q = sum_{abc=q} beta_1(a) beta_2(b) beta_3(c)
objects.pair_Dirichlet_convolution: (F star G)(d,e) := sum_{a|d} sum_{b|e} F(a,b) G(d/a,e/b); Lambda(d,e) is triply well-factorable of pair-level R if for any R_1 R_2 R_3=R there are 1-bounded pair-sequences B_j supported on lcm(d,e)<=R_j such that Lambda = B_1 star B_2 star B_3

new_twin_optimized_pair_weight.parameters: x -> infty, epsilon > 0, R = x^{1/2-epsilon}, factorization R = R_1 R_2 R_3
new_twin_optimized_pair_weight.base_sequence: alpha = rho_1 * rho_2 * rho_3, each rho_j 1-bounded, supported on odd squarefree d<=R_j, rho_j(2)=0
new_twin_optimized_pair_weight.local_damping: for p>=3 set t_p := 1/(p-2), define u(n) := prod_{p|n, p>=3} (1-t_p), u(1)=1, 0<=u(n)<=1, u completely multiplicative on squarefree n
new_twin_optimized_pair_weight.lambda_{d,e}: mu(d) mu(e) alpha(d) alpha(e) u(d) u(e) 1_{(d,e)=1} 1_{2 not | de} 1_{lcm(d,e)<=R}; equivalently: lambda_{d,e} = sum_{m|(d,e)} mu(m) F(d/m) F(e/m), where F(n) := mu(n) alpha(n) u(n) 1_{2 not | n} 1_{n<=R}
new_twin_optimized_pair_weight.W(n): sieve weight: sum_{d|n} sum_{e|n+2} lambda_{d,e} for n<=x

heuristics: By multiplicativity of kappa_p and calibration t_p=1/(p-2), the local p-factor of the first moment of W(n) matches the Hardy-Littlewood twin local factor, yielding the main term proportional to 2C_2, where C_2 = prod_{p>=3}(1-1/(p-1)^2).

The multiplicative damping u(n) imposes a negative bias at each p>=3 against having p|d or p|e (single-hit penalty), matching the local combinatorics of the twin obstruction. The first moment of W(n) matches the twin singular series up to o(1), yielding sum_{n<=x} W(n) ~ (2C_2) x/(log x)^2, where C_2 = prod_{p>=3}(1-1/(p-1)^2).

conjectures.name: pairwise triply well-factorable weight
conjectures.statement:
There exists delta > 0 such that for the weight W(n) above, one has the two-dimensional dispersion bound:
sum_{q <= x^{1/2+delta}} max_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n<=x; n equiv a (mod q)} W(n) - 2C_2 x/(phi(q)(log x)^2)| << x/(log x)^A
for any fixed A > 0, uniformly in x (with constants depending on A and epsilon). Consequently, sum_{n<=x} W(n) = (2C_2+o(1)) x/(log x)^2, and by positivity optimization within the Selberg framework this implies a positive lower bound of the expected order for the weighted twin prime count. Under a two-dimensional Bombieri-Vinogradov hypothesis at level theta > 1/2, this yields an unweighted lower bound:
#{n <= x : n and n+2 prime} >> x/(log x)^2.

conjectures.name: two-dimensional dispersion for triply well-factorable pair weights
conjectures.statement:
There exists delta = delta(epsilon) > 0 such that for the weight W(n) constructed above:
sum_{q <= x^{1/2+delta}} max_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n<=x, n equiv a (mod q)} W(n) - 2C_2 x/(phi(q)(log x)^2)| <<_A x/(log x)^A
for all fixed A > 0. Consequently, sum_{n<=x} W(n) = (2C_2+o(1)) x/(log x)^2, so that, after the standard Selberg-positivity optimization, one obtains a positive lower bound of order x/(log x)^2 for a suitably truncated weighted twin-prime count. Under a two-dimensional Bombieri-Vinogradov hypothesis for the pair sequence at some level theta > 1/2, this yields:
#{n <= x : n and n+2 are prime} >> x/(log x)^2.

There exists delta > 0 (depending on epsilon) such that, uniformly for x large:
sum_{q <= x^{1/2+delta}} max_{(a,q)=1} |sum_{n<=x, n equiv a (mod q)} W(n) - 2C_2 x/(phi(q)(log x)^2)| <<_A x/(log x)^A for every fixed A > 0.
Consequently, sum_{n<=x} W(n) = (2C_2+o(1)) x/(log x)^2. Optimizing the rho_j in a Selberg-positivity framework yields a positive lower bound of order x/(log x)^2 for a truncated weighted twin count. Under a two-dimensional Bombieri-Vinogradov hypothesis at level theta > 1/2 for pair sequences supported by triply well-factorable moduli, this implies:
#{n <= x : n and n+2 prime} >> x/(log x)^2.

Let epsilon > 0 and A > 0. For large x, set Q <= x^{2/3-epsilon}. Let lambda = (lambda_q)_{q>=2} be a triple well-factorable sequence of level Q: there exist functions beta_i: N -> C (i=1,2,3) with supp beta_i subset (Q_i, 2Q_i] and ||beta_i||_infty << (log Q)^{O(1)}, Q_1 Q_2 Q_3 = Q, lambda = beta_1 * beta_2 * beta_3 (Dirichlet convolution), lambda_q = 0 unless 2 <= q <= Q. Write the discrepancy for the cousin-pair correlation:
Delta(x;q,a;4) := sum_{n<=x, n equiv a (mod q)} Lambda(n) Lambda(n+4) - c(q,a;4) x,
where c(q,a;4) := (S(4)/phi(q)) * 1_{(a,q)=1} * 1_{(a+4,q)=1}, and S(4) = 2 prod_{p>2} p(p-2)/(p-1)^2 is the singular series for shift 4. Then, for any A > 0:
sum_{2<=q<=Q} |lambda_q| * max_{a (mod q)} |Delta(x;q,a;4)| <<_{A,epsilon} x (log x)^{-A}.

conjectures.name: Moduli Density
conjectures.statement:
For all but o_epsilon(Q) of such triple-well-factorable moduli q <= Q, there exists a residue class a (mod q) with (a(a+4),q)=1 such that:
sum_{n<=x, n equiv a (mod q)} Lambda(n)Lambda(n+4) = c(q,a;4) x + O(x (log x)^{-A}).

conjectures.name: Strengthened Variance Form
conjectures.statement:
For the same lambda and Q, one has:
sum_{2<=q<=Q} |lambda_q|^2 sum_{a (mod q)} |Delta(x;q,a;4)|^2 <<_{A,epsilon} x^2 (log x)^{-A}.

Fix epsilon, varpi > 0 and A > 0. Let X be large and x in [X,2X], y satisfy X^{2/3+varpi} <= y <= X. Let lambda^{(1)} = (lambda^{(1)}_q)_{q>=2} and lambda^{(2)} = (lambda^{(2)}_r)_{r>=2} be triple well-factorable sequences of levels Q_1 and Q_2: for i=1,2 there exist beta^{(i)}_j: N -> C (j=1,2,3) with supp beta^{(i)}_j subset (Q_{i,j}, 2Q_{i,j}], ||beta^{(i)}_j||_infty << (log X)^{O(1)}, Q_{i,1} Q_{i,2} Q_{i,3}=Q_i, lambda^{(i)}=beta^{(i)}_1*beta^{(i)}_2*beta^{(i)}_3, lambda^{(i)}_m=0 unless 2<=m<=Q_i. Type III x Type III level condition: Q_1 Q_2 <= (Xy)^{2/3-epsilon}. For q,r>=2 and residue classes a (mod q), b (mod r) with (a(a+4),q)=1 and (b(b+4),r)=1, let l := lcm(q,r). If a and b are CRT-compatible, write c = c(a,b) for the unique class mod l; else set Delta(x,y;q,a;r,b;4)=0. Define:
Delta(x,y;q,a;r,b;4) := sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} Lambda(n)Lambda(n+4) - c(l,c;4) y,
where c(l,c;4) := (S(4)/phi(l)) * 1_{(c,l)=1} * 1_{(c+4,l)=1} * prod_{p|l} kappa_p(c;4).
(1) Almost-all short intervals: For all x in [X,2X] outside a set of measure <<_A X(log X)^{-A}:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a (mod q), b (mod r)} |Delta(x,y;q,a;r,b;4)| <<_{A,epsilon,varpi} y(log X)^{-A}.
(2) Mean-square strengthening (variance form):
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r|^2 * sum_{a (mod q)} sum_{b (mod r)} |Delta(x,y;q,a;r,b;4)|^2 <<_{A,epsilon,varpi} y^2 (log X)^{-A}.

Fix epsilon, varpi > 0 and A > 0. Let X be large, x in [X,2X], X^{2/3+varpi} <= y <= X. For i=1,2 let lambda^{(i)} be triple well-factorable sequences of levels Q_i (as described above). Type III x Type III level condition: Q_1 Q_2 <= (Xy)^{2/3-epsilon}. For q<=Q_1, r<=Q_2, residue classes a (mod q), b (mod r) with (a(a+6),q)=1 and (b(b+6),r)=1, l:=lcm(q,r), c the induced class mod l. Define:
Delta(x,y;q,a;r,b;6) := sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} Lambda(n)Lambda(n+6) - c(l,c;6) y,
where S(6) = 2 * C_2 * prod_{p|6, p>2} (p-1)/(p-2), C_2 = prod_{p>2}(1-1/(p-1)^2).
(1) Almost-all short-interval mean bound (un-twisted): For all A > 0, for all x in [X,2X] outside a set of measure <<_A X(log X)^{-A}:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a (mod q), b (mod r)} |Delta(x,y;q,a;r,b;6)| <<_{A,epsilon,varpi} y(log X)^{-A}.
(2) 3-adic twisted equidistribution (Sexy-prime specific): Let chi_3 be the nontrivial Dirichlet character modulo 3. Then for almost all x:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a,b} |sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} Lambda(n)Lambda(n+6) chi_3(n) - chi_3(c) c(l,c;6) y| << y(log X)^{-A}.

conjectures.name: Uniform Split
conjectures.statement:
For many such (q,r), one has:
sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} Lambda(n)Lambda(n+6) (1_{n equiv 1 (mod 3)} - 1_{n equiv 2 (mod 3)}) = o(y/(log X)^2).
This reflects the absence of hidden 3-adic biases for sexy prime pairs.

conjectures.setup:
Fix k>=2 and an admissible k-tuple H = {h_1,...,h_k} subset Z (no prime p has all residues covered by {h_i (mod p)}). Let epsilon, varpi > 0 and A > 0. Let X be large, x in [X,2X], X^{2/3+varpi} <= y <= X. Triple well-factorable weights and Type III x Type III level condition Q_1 Q_2 <= (Xy)^{2/3-epsilon} as above. For 2<=q<=Q_1, 2<=r<=Q_2 and residue classes a (mod q), b (mod r) with (prod_i(a+h_i),q)=1 and (prod_i(b+h_i),r)=1, let l := lcm(q,r). CRT-compatible c denotes the induced class mod l. Main term: c(l,c;H) := (S(H)/phi(l)) * 1_{c+h_i,l=1 for all i} * prod_{p|l} kappa_p(c;H), where kappa_p(c;H) are local corrections normalized so (1/phi(l)) sum_{c (mod l)} c(l,c;H) = S(H)/phi(l). Discrepancy:
Delta_H(x,y;q,a;r,b) := sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} prod_{i=1}^k Lambda(n+h_i) - c(l,c;H) y.
conjectures.statement:
For all A > 0, for all x in [X,2X] outside a set of measure <<_A X(log X)^{-A}:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a (mod q), b (mod r)} |Delta_H(x,y;q,a;r,b)| <<_{A,epsilon,varpi,H} y(log X)^{-A}.

conjectures.name: Small-modulus twisted equidistribution
conjectures.statement:
Let M_0(H) := rad(gcd_{i<j}(h_i-h_j)). For any nontrivial Dirichlet character chi modulo a divisor M|M_0(H), and any (q,r,a,b) as above with M|l, define:
T_chi(x,y;q,a;r,b;H) := sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} (prod_{i=1}^k Lambda(n+h_i)) chi(n+h_1).
Then, for almost all x as above:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a (mod q), b (mod r)} |T_chi(x,y;q,a;r,b;H) - chi(c+h_1) c(l,c;H) y| << y(log X)^{-A}.

conjectures.setup:
Fix a primitive GL(2) newform f of level N (holomorphic or Maass), with nebentypus psi, and let lambda_f(n) denote its normalized Hecke eigenvalues (lambda_f(1)=1, multiplicative, satisfying Ramanujan-Petersson on average). Fix nonzero integer shift h != 0, and parameters epsilon, varpi > 0, A > 0. Let X be large, x in [X,2X], X^{2/3+varpi} <= y <= X. Triple well-factorable weights and Type III x Type III level condition Q_1 Q_2 <= (Xy)^{2/3-epsilon} as above. For 2<=q<=Q_1, 2<=r<=Q_2 and residue classes a (mod q), b (mod r), let l := lcm(q,r). If a and b are CRT-compatible, write c in Z/lZ for the induced class (else set discrepancy to 0). Define:
Delta_{f,h}(x,y;q,a;r,b) := sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} lambda_f(n) lambda_f(n+h).
(For h != 0 the expected main term is 0; for h=0 one expects a positive main term proportional to y/phi(l) from local Rankin-Selberg factors.)
conjectures.statement:
For all A > 0, for all x in [X,2X] outside a set of measure <<_A X(log X)^{-A}:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a (mod q), b (mod r)} |Delta_{f,h}(x,y;q,a;r,b)| <<_{A,epsilon,varpi,f,h} y(log X)^{-A}.
Variance strengthening (L^2 form). For the same x and y:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r|^2 * sum_{a (mod q)} sum_{b (mod r)} |Delta_{f,h}(x,y;q,a;r,b)|^2 <<_{A,epsilon,varpi,f,h} y^2(log X)^{-A}.
Small-modulus twisted equidistribution. Let M|rad(h) and chi be any nontrivial Dirichlet character modulo M, inflated to modulus l. Whenever M|l, define:
T_{f,h,chi}(x,y;q,a;r,b) := sum_{x<n<=x+y, n equiv a (mod q), n equiv b (mod r)} lambda_f(n) lambda_f(n+h) chi(n).
Then, for almost all x as above:
sum_{2<=q<=Q_1} sum_{2<=r<=Q_2} |lambda^{(1)}_q lambda^{(2)}_r| * max_{a (mod q), b (mod r)} |T_{f,h,chi}(x,y;q,a;r,b)| <<_{A,epsilon,varpi,f,h} y(log X)^{-A}.
